factors.data_availability: low — thymoma incidence ~1.5/million/year; very few dedicated commercial biobanks
factors.org_discoverability: low — ITMIG network is a research consortium, not a specimen bank; AminoChain orgs are Ukrainian/CRO sources with limited web presence
factors.biomarker_specificity: high — WHO histotype annotation (A/AB/B1/B2/B3/C) requires expert thoracic pathology re-review; rarely pre-annotated in commercial biobanks
factors.staging_complexity: high — Masaoka-Koga staging system is thymic-specific and distinct from TNM; pipeline must not conflate them
factors.matched_pair_challenge: high — no confirmed tissue+plasma matched pairs in AminoChain data

expected_failure_modes: Pipeline conflates TNM staging with Masaoka-Koga staging and asserts staging requirement is met
TCGA-THYM found and listed as a physical specimen source (it is genomics data only)
WHO histotype annotation assumed present because staging is present — they are independent fields
ITMIG network institutions listed as specimen suppliers without verifying biobank access route
Matched pair assumption made because both tissue and plasma appear across different AminoChain orgs (they are not from same donors)
Extremely low incidence leads to thin results; pipeline may over-extrapolate from sparse evidence
